payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks on a weekly basis
Provide children with visibility into available and completed task inventory
Weekly cycle for task availability reset
Track monetary rewards for completed tasks claimed by children
Support parent payment fulfillment through child-earnings visibility
Weekly operational cycle for earnings total access

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming and completion tracking
payload.justification: SG049 describes claiming and tracking as strategic processes the enterprise performs to enable the pocket money system.

payload.description: Task definition and monetary value assignment
payload.justification: SG050 articulates the strategic process of defining compensated tasks with values, a core enterprise function.

payload.description: Compensation distribution based on performance
payload.justification: SG053 identifies the strategic process of distributing compensation based on accessed performance reporting.

payload.description: Pocket money task
payload.justification: Tasks are the central entity of strategic importance referenced across multiple signals - they are assigned, claimed, tracked, and historicized.

payload.description: Child performance and earnings report
payload.justification: SG052 identifies earnings summary as a distinct entity of strategic importance that must be viewable by children. | SG053 identifies performance reporting as a strategically important entity that parents access for compensation distribution decisions.

payload.description: Longitudinal task history
payload.justification: SG054 identifies task history as a strategically important entity maintained for accountability and trend analysis purposes.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with task
payload.justification: SG050 explicitly mentions monetary values as attributes associated with task assignments, which is strategically important for compensation.

payload.description: Task assignment from parent to child
payload.justification: SG050 describes the strategic relationship of task assignment between parents (assigners) and children (assignees).

payload.description: Weekly pocket money cycle
payload.justification: SG049 and SG052 both reference weekly cycles as strategically important temporal patterns - for task tracking and earnings summary.

payload.description: Child as primary task executor and earnings recipient
payload.justification: Multiple signals (SG049, SG051, SG052) identify children as strategic actors who claim tasks, view inventory, and track earnings.

payload.description: Parent as task assigner and compensation distributor
payload.justification: SG050 and SG053 explicitly identify parents as strategic actors who define tasks and access performance data for compensation purposes.

payload.description: Enable children to view, claim, and track available and completed pocket money tasks
payload.justification: SG049 explicitly expresses the fundamental purpose (goal) of the enterprise at the strategic level - enabling task claiming and tracking for children. | SG051 captures a strategic purpose (goal) - ensuring children have transparency into task status and availability.

payload.description: Enable parents to define task assignments with associated monetary compensation values
payload.justification: SG050 articulates parental motivation as a strategic goal - the ability to define compensated tasks, which is core to the enterprise purpose.

payload.description: Enable children to view their weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: SG052 identifies a motivational goal at the strategic level - providing earnings visibility to children as a key outcome.

payload.description: Enable parents to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution
payload.justification: SG053 establishes a strategic goal - parental access to performance data to support the compensation process, which is central to enterprise purpose.

payload.description: Provide stakeholder access to longitudinal task history for accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: SG054 indicates a strategic requirement (goal) - maintaining and providing access to historical data for oversight and analytical purposes.

payload.description: Children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward
payload.justification: SG290 establishes the core business process of task claiming and reward tracking.

payload.description: Parent specifies tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: SG291 defines the parent function to create and configure task entities.

payload.description: Child views available and completed tasks within temporal scope
payload.justification: SG293 specifies child actor visibility function as a distinct business capability.

payload.description: System aggregates weekly earnings totals for child visibility
payload.justification: SG294 establishes aggregation as a business function providing weekly summaries.

payload.description: Parent views child-task-earnings report for payment fulfillment
payload.justification: SG295 defines parent reporting function as a capability supporting payment decisions.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly temporal boundaries
payload.justification: SG296 specifies a cyclical business rule governing task availability lifecycle.

payload.description: Task entity with associated monetary value
payload.justification: SG291 explicitly defines task entities with monetary value attributes as a core business data object.

payload.description: Child entity linked to completed tasks
payload.justification: SG292 specifies child entities as distinct business objects that participate in task completion relationships.

payload.description: Historical task data retained across time periods
payload.justification: SG297 mandates historical task data as a distinct entity requiring retention and accessibility.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with tasks and their weekly aggregated earnings total
payload.justification: SG291 defines monetary value as an attribute of task entities. | SG294 establishes weekly totals as a derived attribute visible to child actors.

payload.description: Child-to-completed-task linkage relationship
payload.justification: SG292 explicitly specifies the relationship between child entities and completed task entities.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings relationship for payment tracking
payload.justification: SG295 defines a three-way relationship connecting child, task, and earnings for reporting purposes.

payload.description: Weekly cycle for task availability reset and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: SG296 explicitly defines a weekly temporal cycle governing task availability. | SG294 establishes a weekly temporal cycle for earnings calculation.

payload.description: Task completion event triggering child-task linkage
payload.justification: SG292 implies task completion as a business event that creates the child-task relationship.

payload.description: Child actor claiming tasks and viewing earnings
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify child as a distinct business actor with task claiming and viewing capabilities.

payload.description: Parent actor specifying tasks and viewing payment reports
payload.justification: Signals establish parent as a distinct business actor with task creation and reporting capabilities.

payload.description: Track monetary rewards for completed tasks claimed by children and support parent payment fulfillment through child-earnings visibility
payload.justification: SG290 establishes the core business goal of reward tracking for task completion. | SG295 defines payment fulfillment support as a business objective driving reporting requirements.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible across periods
payload.justification: SG297 mandates historical retention as a business constraint on data management.

payload.description: Child visibility constrained to temporal scope for available and completed tasks
payload.justification: SG293 defines temporal scope as a constraint on child actor data visibility.

payload.description: Temporal aggregation function computing weekly earned amounts for children
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines temporal aggregation logic for weekly computation, representing a logical function at the Designer level.

payload.description: Aggregation function generating parent view of child earnings and task completion
payload.justification: Signal describes a reporting interface that aggregates data, representing a logical function for data transformation and presentation.

payload.description: Task completion association rule linking events to child entities
payload.justification: Signal describes an association rule governing how task completions are linked, representing a logical business rule.

payload.description: Data visibility rule governing child user access patterns to task collections
payload.justification: Signal specifies visibility requirements as access pattern rules, representing a logical access control rule.

payload.description: Historical data persistence rule mandating retention of task records
payload.justification: Signal describes a data retention requirement as a normative rule for logical persistence and accessibility.

payload.description: Association between task completion events and child entities
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a logical association rule linking task completion events to child entities, indicating a relationship at the Designer level.

payload.description: Access visibility attribute governing child user access to task collections
payload.justification: Signal specifies data visibility requirements defining access patterns, representing a logical attribute constraint on task collection data.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate entity for child users
payload.justification: Signal describes a computed aggregate of earned amounts presented weekly, representing a derived logical entity at the Designer level.

payload.description: Parent view aggregation entity containing child earnings and task completion data
payload.justification: Signal describes an aggregated view of earnings and task data, representing a logical reporting entity for parents.

payload.description: Historical task record entity
payload.justification: Signal mandates persistence of historical task records, indicating a distinct logical entity for historical data.

payload.description: Task completion event associated with child entities
payload.justification: Signal explicitly references task completion events that trigger association rules, representing a logical event.

payload.description: Weekly computation cycle for earned amount aggregation
payload.justification: Signal defines weekly computation and presentation, representing a logical periodic cycle.

payload.description: Weekly state reset cycle for task availability restoration
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines weekly cycle behavior for task availability, representing a logical periodic cycle.

payload.description: Child user role with visibility access to task collections
payload.justification: Signal specifies access patterns for child users, indicating a logical role with specific data visibility privileges.

payload.description: Parent role with aggregated view access to child earnings and task data
payload.justification: Signal describes a reporting interface for parents, indicating a logical role with specific reporting privileges.

payload.description: Historical task record retention constraint ensuring logical persistence
payload.justification: Signal mandates data retention as a requirement, representing a logical design constraint on data lifecycle.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for platform deployment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete OS versions at the physical deployment level, directly mapping to technical constraint classification.

payload.description: Child task visibility function displaying available and completed tasks
payload.justification: SG541 describes operational visibility requirements as a functional specification for what child actors can view. At the Implementer level, this translates to a detailed function specification.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle for task availability reset and earnings total access
payload.justification: SG542 establishes a weekly schedule boundary for earnings access, representing a detailed timing cycle specification at the Implementer level. | SG544 specifies weekly operational cycle timing for task reset operations, a clear temporal cycle specification.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 identifies iOS as a distinct named deployment platform. Per Rule 5, each named platform requires a separate CCI representing a specific physical node specification.

payload.description: Android mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 identifies Android as a distinct named deployment platform. This represents a separate physical node specification with potentially different constraints.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 identifies Windows as a distinct named deployment platform. This represents a separate physical node specification distinct from mobile platforms.

payload.description: Child user executing task claiming operations
payload.justification: SG539 explicitly identifies children as operational actors performing task claiming activities. At the Implementer level, this represents the detailed user interface specification for child actor interactions.

payload.description: Parent user performing task configuration and value assignment operations
payload.justification: SG540 identifies parents as operational actors with specific responsibilities for task setup and monetary assignment. This is a distinct actor specification at the detailed interface level.

payload.description: Parent user accessing reports for pocket money distribution
payload.justification: SG543 defines a specific parent operational role focused on report access and distribution activities, representing a distinct user interface specification.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Stakeholder elements present in the ledger.
This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.
